title: Die Katherinenhymne des Ricardus Spaldyng und eine Marienhymne derselben Pergamentrolle
summary: CME00071 is the repository XML for a University of Michigan manually keyed and proofread electronic text derived from the text section of Wilhelm Heuser's 1907 Anglia article. The encoding omits Heuser's German manuscript, source, and metrical study on printed pp. 523–538, but retains his editions of two distinct Middle English verse works on pp. 539–548: the twenty-stanza St Katherine hymn attributed through an acrostic to Ricardus Spaldyng, followed by the anonymous five-stanza Hymn on the Five Joys of Mary and its six-line PIPWEL envoy. Both survive in Bodleian Library MS. Bodl. Rolls 22, an unbound parchment roll dated 1400–1410. Heuser reports that the Katherine hymn fills the face and most of the dorse, while the later hand of the Five Joys poem occupies remaining space at the foot of the dorse. A complete 560-page Internet Archive PDF of Anglia volume 30, OCR and structural sidecars are cached. Digital Bodleian currently exposes only one top-of-roll photograph; every canvas in that published selection is cached as partial manuscript coverage, and a mediated whole-roll reproduction route records the need for every exposed face, dorse, membrane, end, join, tab or attachment, and rolled view.
review_notes: The cataloging subject is Heuser's two-poem edited composite, not one homogeneous medieval work and not the complete Anglia article.

The current Bodleian catalog and DIMEV call Richard Spalding the author of the Katherine hymn, but the acrostic does not independently establish whether the name identifies author, patron, or another participant; the attribution remains explicitly qualified.
Physical completeness for this unbound roll is assessed by face, dorse, membranes, ends, joins, attachments, and rolled views, not by codex covers or endleaves.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00071
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow, supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: Internal identifiers IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID are all CME00071.

The 2016-09-21 revision description says a fragment of the German introduction was removed, page breaks and structure were checked, two symbol gaps were added, and three stanza lists were changed to verse lines.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item CME00071
entities.description: A public manually keyed and proofread electronic text selecting the two edited poems, Heuser's headings and notes, and his Göttingen signature from printed pp. 539–548 while omitting the article study on pp. 523–538.

entities.label: Wilhelm Heuser, Die Katherinenhymne des Ricardus Spaldyng und eine Marienhymne derselben Pergamentrolle (1907)
entities.description: Heuser's German-language study and first printed edition of both poems. The article describes the roll and its hands, discusses source and metre, states its normalization and expansion practices, and prints the Katherine hymn on pp. 539–547 and the Five Joys hymn on pp. 547–548; printed p. 547 is shared by the end of the first poem and opening of the second.

bibliographic.citation: Wilhelm Heuser, 'Die Katherinenhymne des Ricardus Spaldyng und eine Marienhymne derselben Pergamentrolle,' Anglia 30, new series 18 (1907), 523–548.
bibliographic.title: Die Katherinenhymne des Ricardus Spaldyng und eine Marienhymne derselben Pergamentrolle

physical_description.extent: printed pp. 523–548 within Anglia volume 30; edited Middle English texts on pp. 539–548
notes: Heuser calls both poems first printings on p. 523. DIMEV gives the article range as pp. 523–548, the Katherine text as pp. 539–547, and visual inspection places the Five Joys text on pp. 547–548 despite a typographical error in DIMEV's subordinate page field.
The article ends with the Five Joys poem, Göttingen dateline, and Heuser signature on printed p. 548; the following scanned page is blank.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS. Bodl. Rolls 22
entities.description: The sole manuscript witness of both poems: an unbound parchment roll. The current catalog assigns the St Katherine hymn to a current Anglicana book hand and the later Five Joys poem to a shaky semitextura hand at the foot of the roll. Heuser reports that the first poem occupies the complete face and most of the dorse, with the second added in the remaining space below it on the dorse.

holding.notes: Held by the Bodleian Library, University of Oxford. The current exact catalog record was substantively revised on 2025-03-20.

physical_description.extent: one roll, 65.75 × 11.25 inches; the public catalog does not enumerate its membranes, joins, tabs, attachments, ends, or rolled views
notes: Item 1 is twenty 14-line stanzas, copied in ten written rows per stanza; item 2 is five eight-line stanzas plus a six-line acrostic envoy.
The St Katherine hymn has colored decoration and initials; the Five Joys initials were lightly drafted but not executed, and its hand is later and less formal.

A complete physical audit must include both face and dorse, every membrane and join, upper and lower ends, any tabs or attachments, and rolled and unrolled views. Codex covers, spine, pastedowns, and endleaves are not applicable unless the repository identifies a separate housing as part of the object.

entities.label: Internet Archive complete provider scan of Anglia volume 30 (1907)
entities.description: A complete 560-page provider PDF of the Robarts Library copy, retaining the binding view, preliminaries, full journal volume, Heuser article, adjacent matter, and terminal blank scans. OCR, page-number, metadata, and scandata sidecars are cached separately.

notes: The volume title page identifies volume 30/new series 18, Halle an der Saale, Max Niemeyer, 1907. The Internet Archive metadata's generic date value 1877 is not used as the volume date.

entities.reproduction_format: PDF page images with provider OCR and structural sidecars

entities.label: Digital Bodleian one-image selection from MS. Bodl. Rolls 22
entities.description: The complete current provider-published selection consists of one color photograph from a 35mm slide, labeled 'top'. It is a cropped detail of the roll opening showing the first two St Katherine stanzas and the beginning of the third; it does not reproduce the complete first poem, any Five Joys text, the lower roll, the dorse, every membrane or join, ends, attachments, or rolled views.

physical_description.extent: one IIIF canvas and one image, provider dimensions 4078 × 2744 pixels; cached access derivative 1800 × 1211 pixels
physical_description.format: One selected color manuscript photograph from a 35mm slide

entities.label: Bodleian Medieval Manuscripts catalog description for MS. Bodl. Rolls 22
entities.description: The current repository description, revised in March 2025, identifying both items, physical form and dimensions, hands, date, origin, competing Pipwel localizations, and the one-image partial Digital Bodleian surrogate.

entities.label: DIMEV 2983: Katherine the courteous of all that I know
entities.description: The Digital Index of Middle English Verse record for the St Katherine hymn, identifying twenty 14-line stanzas, the acrostic attribution to Richard Spaldyng, versification, Bodley Rolls 22 as its manuscript witness, and Heuser's text pages.

entities.label: DIMEV 3498: Mildest of mood and meekest of maidens all
entities.description: The Digital Index of Middle English Verse record for the anonymous Hymn on the Five Joys, identifying five eight-line MARIA stanzas, a six-line PIPWEL acrostic envoy, versification, Sancta Maria as an attributed title, and Bodley Rolls 22 item 2 as the manuscript witness.

local_copies.label: Bodleian Medieval Manuscripts TEI catalog record for MS. Bodl. Rolls 22

access.access_method: Request reference scans through special-collections mediated copying or high-resolution color images through Imaging Services. In the order, request the complete physical roll rather than only the poems: every exposed face and dorse, every membrane, both ends, all joins, tabs or attachments, and rolled and unrolled views, with scale and color target when offered. Identify the work zones separately as the St Katherine hymn from the top through the face and most of the dorse, and the Five Joys poem at the foot of the dorse.
access.format: Reference PDF scans or high-resolution color TIFF images, subject to feasibility, conservation review, quotation, and provider terms
notes: Mediated copying is available to all and currently lists £1 per scan for non-Oxford users. Imaging Services lists a £20 minimum, £27 for the first high-resolution color image and £7.50 for subsequent images from the same item, while large orders are negotiated. Confirm the number of exposures and final quotation before ordering.

The public catalog does not state the number of membranes, joins, or attachments. Ask the repository to enumerate these and confirm that the delivery covers both sides and the complete physical extent.
Ask for the actual order's reuse terms. Research supply or a CC BY-NC grant does not by itself authorize redistribution in a repository connected to commercial or print-on-demand use.

rights.component: Repository XML file and repository-specific markup or additions
rights.basis: The file contains public-domain medieval and 1907 source text, but no file-specific license or complete authorship statement for repository-level XML work was found.
notes: Do not infer a repository-file license solely from the status of its encoded sources.

rights.component: U-M electronic text as presented in the Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse
rights.basis: The U-M collection states that its materials are in the public domain in the United States and displays the No Copyright–United States rights statement.

rights.component: Heuser's 1907 article, edited transcriptions, commentary, apparatus, and typographic presentation
rights.basis: The article was published in 1907, well before the current United States public-domain cutoff for published works.

rights.component: medieval Middle English textual content of both hymns
rights.jurisdiction: United States and jurisdictions with ordinary author-term copyright limits
rights.basis: Both works and the manuscript witness date from the beginning of the fifteenth century, many centuries beyond ordinary copyright terms. This does not determine rights or contractual conditions in modern photography, editions, descriptions, or encodings.

rights.component: Internet Archive PDF, OCR, scan derivatives, metadata, and presentation
rights.basis: The source volume and article are public domain in the United States, but the inspected Internet Archive metadata supplies no item-specific licenseurl or rights field covering every scan derivative and metadata component.
notes: Public download and private caching do not by themselves establish a license for redistribution, repository publication, or print-on-demand reuse of the provider files.

rights.component: Digital Bodleian manuscript photograph and complete cached one-image provider selection
rights.basis: The item-specific IIIF attribution supplies the photograph under Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 International and links Digital Bodleian's terms.
notes: Attribution and noncommercial conditions apply; the license does not authorize commercial or for-profit print-on-demand image use.

Keep the bundle gitignored. Publishing it in a repository intended to enable commercial/POD use requires additional permission or a carefully separated noncommercial use that actually complies with the license.

rights.component: Any future complete-roll reference or high-resolution images supplied by Bodleian copying or Imaging Services
rights.jurisdiction: United Kingdom and use governed by provider terms
rights.basis: Current Bodleian image terms permit specified personal, noncommercial research, teaching, and qualifying academic uses under CC BY-NC 4.0, but restrict commercial uses and direct publication requests through Imaging Services. The actual order and delivery terms may add item-specific conditions.
notes: This is prospective guidance, not a rights statement for a file not yet supplied. Preserve and review the actual invoice, delivery notice, and license.
Private or noncommercial research supply does not authorize public repository redistribution, commercial publication, marketing, covers, or for-profit print-on-demand use.

notes: Facts may be cited and summarized with attribution; keep the cached TEI file gitignored unless redistribution terms are established.

editorial_practices.representation: Regulated capitals for proper names and modern punctuation
editorial_practices.description: Heuser explicitly says he regularized initial capitals for proper names and inserted modern punctuation.
notes: The schema has no combined capitalization-and-punctuation practice value; this record preserves Heuser's one explicit statement and the punctuation component in its description.

editorial_practices.description: Heuser states that abbreviations are expanded and the expansions identified by italic type; the XML preserves these spans with HI REND=italic.
editorial_practices.scope: Both edited poems, printed pp. 539–548 and their CME encoding

editorial_practices.source_feature: Blue and red initials in the Katherine hymn; unexecuted, lightly drafted acrostic initials in the Five Joys poem
editorial_practices.representation: Printed emphasized initials and explanatory prose; XML HI REND=bold spans preserve the print-layer emphasis
editorial_practices.description: Heuser distinguishes the completed colored initials and KATERINA RICARDUS SPALDYNG acrostic in the first poem from reserved or lightly drafted MARIA and PIPWEL initials in the later poem.
editorial_practices.scope: Katherine stanzas 17–19 and the stanza/envoy initials of the Five Joys poem

editorial_practices.source_feature: Complete Heuser article on printed pp. 523–548
editorial_practices.representation: The two edited poems and their notes on pp. 539–548
editorial_practices.description: The U-M encoding omits the German article title and study on pp. 523–538 and begins at the first poem. It retains both poems, shared p. 547, Heuser's footnotes, and his Göttingen signature on p. 548.
editorial_practices.scope: CME00071 as a selected electronic-text unit

evidence.locator: IDG; REVDESCR; TEXT/@LANG; two DIV1 units; PB, HEAD, BYLINE, LG, L, NOTE, HI, GAP, CLOSER, DATELINE, and SIGNED
evidence.summary: Confirms all three internal identifiers, the 2016 revision statement, exact encoded headings and closing, two-work structure, 326 verse-line elements in 26 line groups, 31 notes, ten page breaks for printed pp. 539–548, typography elements, and the part boundary on shared printed p. 547.

evidence.citation: Programmatic and textual inspection of CME00071.xml against the source scan
evidence.locator: two DIV1 elements; 26 LG; 326 L; 31 NOTE; PB N=539–548 / REF=17–26
evidence.summary: Verifies that the XML contains exactly twenty 14-line Katherine stanzas and five eight-line Marian stanzas plus a six-line envoy, and maps the XML's source-page sequence to Heuser's printed text section.
Confirms the stable U-M item identity and public electronic-text access.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, About the Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse
evidence.summary: States that the later CME batches were manually keyed from public-domain editions, proofread and edited in house, and marked up with basic TEI semantics; the collection asserts No Copyright–United States.

evidence.citation: Wilhelm Heuser, 'Die Katherinenhymne des Ricardus Spaldyng und eine Marienhymne derselben Pergamentrolle,' Anglia 30 (1907), 523–548
evidence.locator: article pp. 523–548 / PDF pages 531–556; text preliminaries pp. 538–539; Katherine pp. 539–547; Five Joys pp. 547–548
evidence.summary: Direct inspection verifies the volume title page, article title and endpoint, Heuser's physical account of the roll and side-level poem loci, his acrostic and localization arguments, distinct hands and later addition, editorial capitalization, punctuation, expansions and initials, both complete edited poems, their shared page boundary, and his Göttingen signature.

Confirms the item identifier, Robarts provenance, scanner, complete file inventory, direct PDF and sidecar names, and exact provider file sizes. Its generic date 1877 is contradicted by the inspected 1907 title page and is not propagated.

evidence.citation: Local audit of cached Anglia volume 30 source objects
evidence.locator: 560-page PDF; article PDF pages 531–556; hashes and sizes in access:ia-anglia30
evidence.summary: qpdf reports no syntax or stream errors; PDF metadata reports 560 pages and 33,149,163 bytes. The PDF, metadata, scandata, and page-number sidecars are true hard links to the already verified CME00070 cache; OCR is separately downloaded. Visual inspection confirms the title page, article opening and closing, and shared p. 547 boundary.

evidence.locator: message title, author, container-title, volume, publisher, and DOI
evidence.summary: Confirms Heuser's article title, W. Heuser authorship, Anglia container, 1907 print publication, De Gruyter DOI registration, and DOI.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Medieval Manuscripts in Oxford Libraries, MS. Bodl. Rolls 22
evidence.locator: contents items 1–2; physical description; hands; history; surrogates; revision 2025-03-20
evidence.summary: Confirms current shelfmark and Summary Catalogue number, two distinct poem items, incipits and explicits, stanza/acrostic structures, parchment roll form and dimensions, distinct hands and later addition, date 1400–1410 and England origin, competing Pipewell Abbey versus Pipwell Manor interpretations, and that Digital Bodleian supplies only one image from 35mm slides.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian IIIF Presentation manifest for MS. Bodl. Rolls 22
evidence.locator: one sequence, one canvas labeled top, one Image API service; attribution and terms
evidence.summary: Confirms a one-canvas 4078 × 2744-pixel provider selection from a 35mm slide, exact Image API service, Bodleian attribution, and item-specific CC BY-NC 4.0 terms. It does not support whole-roll coverage.

evidence.citation: Visual and technical audit of the complete one-image Digital Bodleian selection
evidence.summary: ZIP integrity passes. The inventory records the exact /full/1800,/0/default.jpg request; the returned image is 1800 × 1211 pixels. Visual comparison with Heuser's text identifies complete stanzas 1–2 and the opening of stanza 3 in the top crop. The selection contains no Five Joys text and no dorse or lower-roll coverage.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse, DIMEV 2983
evidence.locator: record identity, description, author, subjects, versification, manuscript witness, first and last lines, editions
evidence.summary: Identifies the St Katherine hymn as twenty 14-line stanzas with acrostics, associates the name Richard Spaldyng, gives rhyme scheme ababababccdccd, identifies Bodley Rolls 22 as the sole witness, and maps Heuser's edition to article pp. 523–548 and text pp. 539–547.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse, DIMEV 3498
evidence.locator: record identity, description, subjects, versification, manuscript witness, attributed title, first and last lines, editions
evidence.summary: Identifies the anonymous Five Joys hymn as five eight-line MARIA stanzas plus a six-line PIPWEL acrostic envoy, gives rhyme scheme ababbcbc, records Sancta Maria as an attributed title, and identifies Bodley Rolls 22 item 2 as its sole witness. The record's subordinate Heuser text-page field reads 257–258, contradicted by direct inspection of printed pp. 547–548; the error is not propagated.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Special collections mediated copying
evidence.summary: States that the service is available to all, supplies reference PDF scans, accepts an order form, charges non-Oxford users £1 per scan, may decline items for conservation or legal reasons, and applies Imaging Services terms.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Order images from Imaging Services
evidence.summary: Gives the current high-resolution color-image prices, £20 minimum, large-order quotation process, technical TIFF specifications, order-form route, contacts, and separation between research supply and publication/reuse permission.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian Terms of Use and Bodleian Imaging Services Terms of Use
evidence.summary: States that most Digital Bodleian images are CC BY-NC 4.0 with attribution, defines permitted noncommercial research and qualifying academic uses, restricts commercial use without permission and fees, and directs publication or licensing requests to Imaging Services. Actual item-specific terms remain controlling.

open_questions.question: Can Bodleian Libraries supply a complete, auditable digital facsimile of MS. Bodl. Rolls 22 rather than the current single top detail?
next_steps: Ask mediated copying or Imaging Services for the complete physical roll: every exposed face and dorse, all membranes, both ends, every join, tab or attachment, and rolled and unrolled views, with scale and color target where feasible.
Ask the repository to state the number and order of membranes and whether any housing, detached element, blank area, or attachment belongs to the physical object.
Keep the St Katherine zone and Five Joys zone separately mapped inside the complete delivery, and record the actual order's redistribution, publication, and commercial-use terms.
notes: The negative finding covers the current exact catalog, Digital Bodleian object, and one-canvas IIIF manifest. It is dated and is not proof that no other institutional photography exists.

open_questions.question: What role does the KATERINA RICARDUS SPALDYNG acrostic identify with sufficient confidence: author, commissioner, guild member, or another participant?
next_steps: Consult Ruth Kennedy's full 2003 critical edition and 2004 guild study, compare their authorial and scribal arguments with the complete roll, and retain the repository/DIMEV author attribution as attributed rather than certain meanwhile.

open_questions.question: Does the PIPWEL acrostic point to Pipewell Abbey in Northamptonshire, Pipwell Manor near Holbeach in south Lincolnshire, a person, or another association?
next_steps: Review the full Kennedy studies and inspect the complete dorse, drafted initials, hands, and any joins or ownership evidence; preserve the current catalog's competing attributions rather than turning either into a confirmed composition place.

open_questions.question: What item-level U-M production record explains the selection of pp. 539–548 and the 2016 removal of a German introductory fragment?
next_steps: Ask U-M Digital Content and Collections for item-level source-selection, keying, and revision records while preserving the directly observed XML-to-print mapping.
